settings.text: These are the most commonly asked questions about Hydro Pro

settings.text: Our goal is for every customer to be totally satisfied with their purchase. If this isn't the case, let us know and we'll do our best to work with you to make it right.

These are the most commonly asked questions

settings.heading: What is hydrogen water?

settings.text: Hydrogen water is regular drinking water infused with extra molecular hydrogen gas (H₂). Think: water + a fresh dose of hydrogen you can actually use.

settings.heading: What does "PPB" mean?

settings.text: PPB = parts per billion. It's how we measure how much dissolved hydrogen (H₂) is in your water after a cycle. Higher PPB = stronger hydrogenation.

settings.heading: How does a hydrogen bottle/pitcher work?

settings.text: Dr. Water uses SPE/PEM-style electrolysis to generate molecular hydrogen and dissolve it into the water. You'll literally see bubbles during the cycle — that's hydrogen being infused.

settings.heading: Isn't hydrogen already in water (H₂O)? Why add more?

settings.text: In H₂O, hydrogen is chemically bound to oxygen — it doesn't behave like free molecular hydrogen gas (H₂). Hydrogen water adds free H₂ that dissolves into the water and is consumed fresh.

settings.heading: I didn't find my question — what now?

settings.text: Message support with a photo/video if it's a technical issue. The fastest fixes happen when we can see what you're seeing.

settings.heading: What's your return policy?

settings.text:
60 Day Money Back Guarantee

Try it in real life. If you don't love it, reach out — we'll guide you through an easy returzn based on the policy shown at checkout.

settings.heading: What warranty do you offer?

settings.text: A standard limited warranty covers manufacturing defects. If something isn't right, we fix it — quickly and fairly.

settings.heading: Is this third‑party tested / certified?

settings.text: Yes — Dr. Water products are built with safety and performance in mind, and select products have third‑party documentation available. If you ever want to verify something, our support team can share the relevant report.

settings.heading: How do I charge the HydroTumbler — and how long does it last?

settings.text: Charge with USB‑C. A full charge typically supports many cycles (most users go about a week depending on usage). If you use it multiple times daily, charge more often — like your phone.

settings.heading: How do I clean it without damaging the hydrogen system?

settings.text: Weekly: run a gentle clean using a diluted white‑vinegar rinse, then run 1–2 cycles with clean water to flush. Avoid harsh soaps, bleach, or abrasive cleaners on the hydrogen unit.

settings.heading: How strong is Dr. Water hydrogen — and what's the difference between products?

settings.text:
HydroTumbler: up to ~3000 PPB in ~10 minutes (portable, rechargeable). HydroPitcher: up to ~1500 PPB in ~10 minutes (family-size, countertop plug-in).
Hydrator Pro: Up to 5140PPB - Verified by H2Analytics

settings.heading: How long does hydrogen stay in the water?

settings.text: Hydrogen is light — it escapes fast. For the best experience, drink it within 15–30 minutes of the cycle finishing. Keep it sealed and cold to slow the fade.

settings.heading: Can I use tap water?

settings.text: Yes — but if your goal is better taste + better performance + longer device life, use filtered or RO water when you can. Chlorine/minerals can shorten the lifespan of hydrogen-generating components over time.

settings.heading: What kind of water gives the best results?

settings.text: Filtered or RO water is the "clean base." It improves taste, helps protect the internals, and typically gives more consistent hydrogen levels.

settings.heading: Can I run a cycle with hot water?

settings.text: We recommend hydrogenating cool or room‑temperature water for best performance. If you want warm water, hydrogenate first, then warm it separately.

settings.heading: Can I add electrolytes, lemon, flavor drops, or supplements?

settings.text: Not during hydrogenation. Add-ons can interfere with the system and leave residue. If you want flavors/supplements: hydrogenate first → pour into a separate cup → add your mix.

settings.heading: What's the simplest daily routine?

settings.text: Morning: run a cycle → drink fresh. Midday: refill → run a cycle → sip. Workout: use your tumbler pre‑ and post‑session. Night: quick cycle if you want your "clean reset" before bed.

settings.heading: What are the benefits people usually notice?

settings.text: Most people buy hydrogen water for cleaner hydration, and many report feeling more consistent energy, better recovery, and a lighter "less sluggish" feeling when they stay consistent. (Hydration habits matter more than "one perfect glass.")

settings.heading: Is hydrogen water safe to drink every day?

settings.text: For most healthy adults, yes — it's still water. If you're pregnant, nursing, or managing a medical condition, check with your clinician (especially if you're changing hydration/supplement routines).

settings.heading: How much should I drink per day?

settings.text: There's no single magic number. A practical approach: start with 1–2 servings/day, then build consistency. Your body will tell you more than marketing will.
